creator: iwtcex@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Gerald Pfeifer from comment #9)

> Point taken on these two aspects, Vulkan and D3D12,
> not being very expensive, but they do additional dependencies.

I don't buy this argument. Other than vulkan-loader (a tiny library), Vulkan has exactly the same dependencies as OpenGL.

> The question with Wine partly is one of users -
> game/highly graphical users versus ones that just want to run
> an older Window app, for example (and no Gamepad support, say).

Well, is there any demand specifically for "no # support"? If these
features do not affect those users, why should they care?
